name: I slipped at work in Murray but didn't tell anyone right away, can I still file workers comp?
description: Yes, file soon in Utah despite delay, but report immediately to employer/Labor Commission and gather evidence to counter credibility issues.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: I slipped at work in Murray but didn't tell anyone right away, can I still file workers comp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, file soon in Utah despite delay, but report immediately to employer/Labor Commission and gather evidence to counter credibility issues.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, you can still file a workers' compensation claim in Utah even if you didn't report immediately, but delays make approval harder due to proof and credibility issues.[1][2] Utah requires reporting within a specific timeframe, often 180 days, but immediate notification is best to document the injury and avoid denials.[1][2] Key factors: (1) Report as soon as possible to your employer or Utah Labor Commission if they won't; (2) Gather any evidence like photos or witness statements; (3) Delays raise questions about work-relatedness; (4) Insurers scrutinize late claims for pre-existing issues.[1][2] Document everything now, contact the Labor Commission, and avoid discussing details with coworkers to prevent complications. Consult a specialist promptly to evaluate your case strength.
